pattern: Treating an integer as a fixed 32-bit field lets you extract each bit from the low end and assemble the mirror image at the high end in a single pass.
hints: Each bit in position i of the input should end up at position 31-i of the output — how do you read one bit at a time from the input and place it correctly in the result?
Shift the result left by 1 before ORing in the next bit, and shift the input right by 1 to advance to the next bit — repeat exactly 32 times regardless of leading zeros.
In Python, format(n, '032b') gives a 32-character binary string; reversing that string and converting back with int(..., 2) is a clean one-liner that makes the bit-reversal intention explicit.

edge_inputs.description: single low bit set becomes high bit
all 32 bits set except bit 0 (which is always 0 since n is even) — reversed is all set except bit 31
power of two: only one bit, moves to mirror position

stress.description: random even integers across the full allowed range
